scope: resident chunked matrix-warp metadata upload reuse

native_binding.behavior: index and inverse metadata are prepared/uploaded once for the full batch; output and coverage scratch remain chunk-capacity bounded; each chunk uses offset device metadata pointers

resident_memory_model.behavior: planned chunked-warp output/coverage bytes remain capacity-bound, while planned index/inverse bytes now cover all planned warp frames

devices.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition

first_attempt.failure: focused CUDA tests caught wrong output because scatter still used the unoffset full index pointer for later chunks
first_attempt.fix: pass workspace.indices.get() + begin to the scatter kernel, matching the warp kernel's metadata offset

memory_model.assertion: planned output/coverage bytes are capacity-bound while planned inverse/index bytes cover full planned warp frame count

resident_artifact.assertion: resident registration artifact records single metadata upload mode/counts for default chunked warp

real_200_light_baseline.reason: C: drive had about 0.17 GiB free during validation; this gate validates native upload-count behavior with synthetic CUDA tests.
real_200_light_baseline.input: M38 H-alpha, 200 lights, 20 bias, 20 dark, 20 flat
real_200_light_baseline.expected_metadata_upload_reduction: from one index+inverse upload per chunk to one index+inverse upload per native batch call

notes: No official PixInsight WBPP/PJSR source was read or used.
WBPP evidence remains limited to user-generated black-box timing/output metadata from earlier accepted baseline artifacts.
Input image directories were not modified.

known_limitations: Warp, coverage-reduce, and scatter kernels still launch once per chunk.
The gate reduces metadata upload orchestration but does not fuse chunk kernels.
A new real 200-light run was skipped because C: free space was too low for a large output directory.
next_gate_recommendation: Reduce resident warp per-chunk launch overhead, likely by fusing coverage/scatter work or moving toward matrix-warped integration for accepted registration matrices.
